{{DISPLAYTITLE:'''SPACEGRAVE'''}} {{Class 3|safety=Unsafe|security=Unstable|color-l=100%||color-s=100%}} {{Underline|1=ffffff}} {{Underline|1=ffffff}}

'''Level 401''' is the 402nd level of the Backrooms. ------------ {{Audio|align=center|file=Space Background ambience.mp3|volume=0.15|=yes}} ------------
== Description == '''Level 401''' features the key components of an outer space environment seamlessly integrated into various hallways and spaces, all bathed in a noir aesthetic. The floors and walls in this level are presumed to be made of concrete, coated with a thin layer of latex. The level is illuminated by white light sources, seemingly producing from the ceiling. The specific scientific nature of these light sources is undisclosed. The floor and walls do not confine themselves to traditional boundaries, rather, they extend to encompass diverse environments such as caves, waterfalls, lakes, seas, mountains, ruins, hallways, hotels and even serene theaters. Across the level, marble spikes seem to emerge, dispersed in specific places such as tunnels, cave entrances, and more. Sporadically, supplies of food and water are found within the level, either independently or in conjunction with colonies and outposts. The level is devoid of any form of communication, whether can it be radio, television, cell phone, etc. Extended travel through this level often induces a sense of madness in most individuals, attributed to the incessant, pulsating background ambient. At times, the background is punctuated by the sounds of falling objects or impacts. This phenomenon may be attributed to either low gravity conditions or entities that unintentionally propel objects into the surroundings. Frostbite serves as an exemplar for these entities. Further details will be provided later on. '''Level 401''' is teaming with various outer space artifacts, ranging from remnants of spaceships and deposits of titanium ore to white neon stars and more. Upon through investigation, K.R.O. (Kauer Research Organization) has concluded that these artifacts are remnants of a colossal spaceship crash, potentially serving as the catalyst for the level's existence. The spaceship measured approximately 5 km2 in width and 500m in height. The level presents a colder environment than anticipated, with temperatures standing around -15°C (5°F). The rational behind this climate is the altitude, situated at approximately 2500 meters above sea level, resulting in thinner air and low gravity. This temperature variation may result in hypothermia and an increased risk of death.
ATTENTION! You have left an unsaved audio log. {{Collapse|num=4|initial='''Open?'''|final='''Close?'''
|body=
{{POI|name=Harper Mitchell|status=Missing|location=Level 401|affliations=Unknown|seen=Level 401}}
Day 1

[ LOG START ]
[Heavy breathing, disoriented] "I... I don't even know where I am, man. It's like... some sort of outer-space, or something. Can't see a damn thing. it's dark as hell." [Sound of footsteps, as Harper seemingly starts walking] "Just had this thought about sliding down that thing, and now I'm here?! It's..." [A moment of silence, distant sounds of unknown origin] "..I don't even know how to put this into words. It's like... seriously unreal." [Whispers in the background, indistinct and eerie] "--WHAT THE̶̺̜̬̜͗̄̐ ̷̧͎̖̩͕͓̯̥̫̑̆͐͊͑̆̉̂͒̄̆͛̈́͂͝F̸̫̼̱͓͖̒̕͘" [END OF THE LOG]
----------------
Day 2

[ LOG START ] "... I stumbled on something. I don't even know what it was." [Harper stood up, shuffling and picking up some scattered items] "And it broke my audio log! I already hate this place." [Exasperated sigh, followed by a brief pause] "Guess I should've expected the unexpected in this cosmic mess. Can't shake the feeling there's more to this than it meets the eye. Maybe some kind of experimental zone? Or maybe... a portal gone wrong? Dammit, Harper, focus. Figure this out, get out. As simple as that!.." [END OF THE LOG] ----------------
Day 3

[ LOG START ] [Heavy breathing and shuffling] "Okay... I don't know if anyone's gonna hear this, but I need to document what's happening. I've been stuck in this place for days now, I think?! No exit, nothing. It's freezing, and I'm seriously starting to lose it here. I just want to... [sigh] get out of here." [Pause] [Whispering] "What is this level supposed to be? It's like some messed-up maze or something. I can't take it anymore." [Silence, followed by faint footsteps] "Wait... What was that? Did you hear that? Hold on, I'm gonna check it out." [More shuffling, then a sudden gasp] "..What the heck is that thing? I can't even describe it. It's just... [stammering] unnatural. I need to get out of here." [Pause, footsteps backtracking] "I don't know if this recording will make it out, but if someone finds it, just know I saw something... something not right." [Panicked breathing] "I'm running. I'M RUNNING- OH MY GO--" [ END OF THE LOG ] -----------------
Day 8
[Background human talking] ".. 8. Can you believe it? 8 days stuck in this hellhole. Christ..." "(Oi! Get over here, it's your turn!)" "Coming!!" [Harper stood up, shuffling and heading in a direction] "But before I bit the dust, those M.E.G. guys snagged me and dragged me into this mess... I'm praying they'll actually figure out how to get me out of this place." [A thunderous explosion echoes in the distance] "..The heck?.." ... ... [Fast and thunderous footsteps are heard, increasing in volume] "--HOLY SHIT-" [ END OF THE LOG ] ---------------- [ START OF THE LOG ] " ̵̡̥̅̓͐̋̋̓̿̔̂͋̍͠͠͠ ̷̢̧̛̗̦͇̳̟͉̺͉̖̺͍͔͐̈̆̏̚ͅ ̸̤̼͔͍̘̮̼̫̺̜̺̹̒̑͋̽̍̊̓͊̅͌͝ ̶͇͇̮̩̟̑̉ ̸̹̠̬̖̓ ̷̺̯̝̬̫̙̹̥͔͊ ̶̨̧͔͈̳̭̖̤̯̦̯̳̂̓͑́̈̏́̒́̌̈͜͜ͅ ̷̧̛̺͓͇̙̠̯͎̻̺̥̺͎̰͠ ̷̢̭̺̪̬̝͚͛̒͊͗ ̷̻͙͓̞̟̖̏̃́̍͘ͅ ̷̝̖͍̺͍̜̗͕͓̆͌̾̊͌͐͘ ̷̢̡̦̱͍͉͉̯̫̥̫̈́͌̈̈́̌͋̇͘͜ ̷̛̭͒̿̍̏̈́͗̕ ̴̡̬͇̘̺̼͉̎̓͆̔̀̀̇͑̈́͛͊̀͝͝ͅ ̵̡̧͇̯̪̞̘̀̕ ̸̞͑̽͂̚͠ ̸̪͈̬̓̽̈́̽͊͊̑́͋̑̕̕͝ ̵̡̢̖̘͍͎̜̪̬̗͙̙̰̾̆̐̍̏͠͠ ̷̛̛̭͔̅̓̎̒̾̓̈́̀͛̊̌ ̸̫͚̝͍͇͓̤̀̐̎̿́̓̉̌̒ ̶̪̖̦̪̩̣̞͔̪̫͕̅ ̶̢̢͕͚̱͔̖̜̘̜̺͎͖̬͖̀̀̈̓̄̔̌͛͐͋̉̎ ̶̛͓̰̙͚̦̱̱̟̥̐̄͆͂̊͜͜ͅ ̵̧̮̃̀͋͗̉ " [ END OF THE LOG ] -------------------- [ START OF THE LOG ] " ̸̛̮̳̟̱͓͚͖͓̞̺͙̣͙̎͋̏̓͑́̎͂ ̶̨̝͖̯̲̠̦̖̠̲̝͍̗̩̯͊̎̿̈́ ̸̛̞͎̜̳͕̞̙̑̇̏̿͂̾̓͛̆̓͆͜ ̸̣̟̗̺͎̯͗̋́͜͠ ̶̛̘͚͔̬̟͖̣͈̩͕͇̳̟̗̬̋̃̆͊̈́͊̈̊̃̓̊̀͠͝ ̵̗͓̣̼͙͖͉͖̟̙̣̬̌̉̈́̑̈́ ̶̢̳̟͙̺̼͐̌̏̑́͝ ̵̫̘̗̘̣̠͙͎̰̘̣̤̱͛͝ ̶̝̓͂͐͑̇̈͆͒̄̊̌ ̶̠̩͂̅̉̑̿͗̊͋̉͠ ̸̤̗͖͉͇͛̽̅̑̂̑͐͐̈́͐̓̇̓̚͠ ̵̡̹̹̖̜̙̦̹̞̹̎̅͆̾͜͝ ̵̢̨̡̫̻̫̻̹̩͌̌̊͜ͅ ̷̧̳̹͎̺̭͖͈̹̣̂͜ ̸̱̓̒͐́ ̷̛̗͚̹͚̤̞͔̞̊̂ " [ END OF THE LOG ] ------------------- [ START OF THE LOG ] ... ... ... ... ------------------
[ FINAL LOG ]
[Background ambient noise - faint wind, distant echoes] [Heavy, somber tone] "It's been silent for two weeks since this tape has started. No signs of life, no communication. We're left with the haunting uncertainty of the poor guy's fate. It's an eerie conclusion, leaving us in the shadows of the unknown. The tape has ended. We're forced to make the grim acknowledgment that Harper is either gone or lost in the void. May this recording serve as a testament to the enigmatic and perilous nature of the cosmos that claimed Harper. In the absence of closure, we hold onto the hope that, somehow, Harper finds his way back from the silence that engulfs this tape. This is the final log." [ END OF LOG ]
}} ==Entities== {{Collapse|initial=
Open entries on FROSTBITE.
|num=1|final=
Close entries on FROSTBITE.
|body=
Frostbite
Frostbite comprises two large, deformed arms extending into the obscurity, exclusively found within Level 401. Any contact with these hands leads to an icy transformation, and those affected succumb to death within seconds. Despite their seeming indifference to living creatures, they seamlessly merge with the darkness upon detecting their presence. The hands are thought to be crafted from marble; however, inflicting even a minor scratch on them demands significant effort.
}}{{Collapse|initial=
Open entries on WALKERS.
|final=
Close entries on WALKERS.
|body=
Walkers
Aside of '''[[Entity 10|Skin-Stealers]]''', '''[[Entity 3|Smilers]]''' and '''[[Entity 9|Facelings]]''', the level is mostly filled with such entities called Walkers. Walkers are shadowy figures that wander throughout the Level 401. They appear to be very aggressive and hostile towards the living creatures most of the time. The extent of their abilities is still unclear, as they primarily target individuals in solitary encounters. However, after thorough investigation, it becomes evident that they possess significant strength and speed. Their exceptional physical abilities may be attributed to exposure to extraterrestrial modifications or enhancements.
|num=2}} ==Colonies and Outposts== Despite the hazardous environment of the level, it is still possible to establish outposts, yet in limited numbers. ==='''[[M.E.G.]]''' Austronatic Colony=== M.E.G. Austronatic Colony is a settlement with a total of 23 members. Their primary mission is to assist travelers to and within the tunnels, eventually guiding them to the exit. ==Entrances and Exits == ===Entrances=== *No-clipping through a glitched rift in '''[[Level 1]]''' may teleport you here. *Jumping into a gap in the '''[[Level 117]]''' may drop you here. ===Exits=== *Going through an exit tunnel may lead you to '''[[Level 37]]'''. ----
